title: Final Fantasy VII Remake
reason: Liked FFVII original and wants modern action combat with strong story and aesthetics.
next_action: Check platform path before prioritizing.

title: Final Fantasy VII Rebirth
reason: Liked FFVII original and wants emotionally strong character arcs with modern combat.
next_action: Treat as a follow-up after Remake if access is possible.

title: NieR: Automata
reason: Fits tragic and emotionally complex storytelling if the early confusion barrier is acceptable.
user_feedback: User already started it and paused early.
next_action: Reassess after a guided re-entry recommendation.

title: Tales of Arise
reason: Action JRPG with clear progression and relatively fast engagement.
next_action: Use when prioritizing clarity and accessibility.

title: Stranger of Paradise: Final Fantasy Origin
reason: Could fit if combat engagement matters more than narrative polish.
next_action: Recommend only if a gameplay-first mood is explicit.

title: NEO: The World Ends with You
reason: Very strong fit based on love for the original TWEWY, stylish presentation, party combat, Shibuya identity, and faster modern action flow.
user_feedback: Played a lot and liked it, but it felt repetitive at times and the user is not fully sure whether the ending was played or watched.
next_action: No further action needed.

title: Persona 5 Royal
reason: Strong fit for style, party bonds, progression, world identity, and emotional turns, with some pacing risk due length.
user_feedback: Owned and started, but the opening felt slow.
next_action: Recommend only when in the mood for a slower burn.

title: Metaphor: ReFantazio
reason: High fit for fantasy worldbuilding, bonds, buildcraft, class systems, and political stakes.
next_action: Prioritize on Series S.

title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33
reason: High fit for tragic tone, party bonds, strong art direction, and satisfying reactive combat/build systems.
user_feedback: Lowered difficulty to easy, liked the atmosphere and gameplay somewhat, but quit on a Renoir fight and watched the rest.
next_action: Do not prioritize similar difficulty-spike RPGs without checking accessibility first.

title: 13 Sentinels: Aegis Rim
reason: High fit for branching-feeling mystery, major story reframes, and strong emotional payoff, though the combat style is more specialized.
user_feedback: Completed and liked a lot for concept, story, mystery, and gameplay structure.
next_action: No further action needed.

title: Unicorn Overlord
reason: Likely fit for tactical progression, worldbuilding, and strong art direction, but with some risk if the emotional anchor feels too light.
user_feedback: Played a lot, but it became repetitive and is now on hold.
next_action: Revisit only if in the mood for more systems-first tactical play.

title: Scarlet Nexus
reason: Could fit if the goal is fast stylish combat, bond episodes, and strong audiovisual identity over deeper political worldbuilding.
next_action: Use when prioritizing action and momentum on Series S.

title: Xenoblade Chronicles 3
reason: Very strong fit for tragic group dynamics, emotional payoff, class/build systems, and long-form party bonding.
next_action: Prioritize on Switch if you want a big commitment with strong emotional arc.

title: Fire Emblem: Three Houses
reason: Strong fit for route-based character attachment, political conflict, and tactical progression, with some pacing risk in academy sections.
user_feedback: Completed one route, invested many hours, and watched the other routes.
next_action: No further action needed.

title: TRIANGLE STRATEGY
reason: Strong fit for political stakes and meaningful decisions, but with real risk from slower early pacing.
user_feedback: Completed and loved it without complaints.
next_action: No further action needed.

title: Ys VIII: Lacrimosa of DANA
reason: Strong fit for action momentum, party journey, satisfying progression, and emotional payoff.
next_action: Prioritize on Switch if you want something faster than a typical long JRPG.

title: Ni no Kuni II: Revenant Kingdom
reason: Reasonable fit if you want more Ni no Kuni world and smoother action, but it may land weaker than the first on emotional depth.
user_feedback: Owned/in backlog, but paused for unclear reasons.
next_action: Revisit only when in the mood for a lighter action JRPG.

title: OCTOPATH TRAVELER II
reason: Good fit for systems and presentation, but more fragmented storytelling makes it a risk versus your stronger preference for cast cohesion.
next_action: Recommend only if combat systems and HD-2D presentation are the main draw.

title: Persona 3 Reload
reason: Likely fit for style, bonds, emotional payoff, and mystery, but it carries the same slow-start risk already visible with Persona 5 Royal.
next_action: Prioritize on Series S only if you want a slower burn with strong payoff.

title: Yakuza: Like a Dragon
reason: Very strong fit for party bonds, dramatic turns, strong progression systems, and a lovable underdog cast, with enough emotional anchor to offset its side content sprawl.
user_feedback: Started it, but it is still pending.
next_action: Revisit when in the mood for a longer party RPG with strong story and progression.

title: Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth
reason: Potentially excellent fit for emotional payoff and party dynamics, but it should come after Yakuza: Like a Dragon for full impact.
next_action: Recommend only after finishing Yakuza: Like a Dragon.
